name: DiRAC - Extreme Scaling (Tursa)
oneLiner: DiRAC's extreme-scaling GPU service, built for giant parallel workloads in physics and AI-adjacent simulation.
summary: DiRAC's Tursa service at EPCC is the programme's extreme-scaling platform and the most overtly accelerator-heavy DiRAC installation. It combines A100 GPU nodes, CPU support nodes, very large RAM, 5.1 PB of Lustre storage, and non-blocking HDR InfiniBand in water-cooled racks. The service is designed for demanding particle-physics and cosmology workloads, but the same characteristics make it suitable for AI inference or training tasks that need large parallel GPU estates.

keyFacts.hardware: 64 nodes with 4 NVIDIA A100 80 GB GPUs, 114 nodes with 4 NVIDIA A100 40 GB GPUs, plus CPU nodes and 5.1 PB Lustre
keyFacts.performance: 291,840 Tensor cores with non-blocking HDR InfiniBand
keyFacts.energy: Water-cooled racks for dense accelerator deployment

access.route: Projects apply via the DiRAC Resource Allocation Committee; trial and commercial access are also possible.
